name: Joe Guy
abstract: Joseph Luke Guy (September 29, 1920 – June 1, 1962) was an American jazz trumpeter. He had a promising career as a young, progressive be-bop musician as he worked alongside more prominent musical acts until a drug addiction sidelined him from further success.

concerts.label: Carnegie Pop Concert: Jazz at the Philharmonic

works.t: No No Nanette: Tea for Two

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: The band consisted of Monk on piano, Kermit Scott, tenor player, that's up in San Francisco now, Joe Guy on trumpet, Kenny Clarke on drums, and a bass player named Nick Penton.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: The Coleman Hawkin's orchestra was composed of Joe Guy and Tommy Lindsay... The only musicians in the band that I had known before going to Europe were Jackie Fields and Gene Rodgers.

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Eldridge
about_quotes.evidence: And I came to the Savoy, and all the cats used to come up there and sit in -- Dizzy, Joe Guy, Charlie, Bobby Moore, all the little trumpet players that was around in those days.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: It was Joe Guy, Monk, bass player Nick Fenton... And it was like a hang out too, you know. We would go there... I used to go down there and play with them

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Rowles
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee does not mention Joe Guy; the mention comes from the Interviewer stating 'some people say that was... She started going with Joe Guy'.

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Great trumpet player, Joe Guy -- I did a lot of dates, single dates with these guys... They'd have a 12 piece band, you know.
